findings.title: TeamPCP + Vect RaaS + BreachForums: 300K Affiliates Mass Mobilization
findings.summary: TeamPCP has formally partnered with Vect ransomware-as-a-service and BreachForums to distribute personal Vect affiliate keys to all ~300,000 BreachForums users, creating an unprecedented supply-chain-to-RaaS industrialized attack pipeline.

findings.title: LAPSUS$ Claims 3GB AstraZeneca Breach via TeamPCP Stolen Credentials
findings.summary: LAPSUS$ publicly claims a 3GB breach of AstraZeneca using credentials stolen via TeamPCP-compromised packages, representing the first named victim of the TeamPCP/LAPSUS$ partnership and offering access for sale via Session messaging.

findings.title: CVE-2026-21858 'Ni8mare' — n8n Unauthenticated RCE CVSS 10.0
findings.summary: A Content-Type confusion bug in n8n's webhook middleware enables unauthenticated remote code execution on an estimated 100,000 globally deployed n8n servers; a public exploit chain (chained with CVE-2025-68613) is live and PoC is trending on GitHub.

findings.title: TeamPCP Telnyx PyPI Compromise — WAV Steganography Credential Stealer
findings.summary: TeamPCP pushed malicious telnyx versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 to PyPI using WAV audio steganography to conceal a cross-platform credential harvester that exfiltrates to 83.142.209[.]203:8080, with Windows persistence via msbuild.exe in the Startup folder.

findings.title: CVE-2025-53521 — F5 BIG-IP Added to CISA KEV, Federal Patch Deadline March 30
findings.summary: CISA added CVE-2025-53521 affecting F5 BIG-IP to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on March 27, 2026, with a mandatory federal patch deadline of March 30 — all organizations with internet-facing BIG-IP appliances should treat this as emergency priority.

findings.title: LangChain + LangGraph: Three Enterprise Data Exposure CVEs (CVSS up to 9.3)
findings.summary: Three patched CVEs in LangChain/LangGraph expose enterprise secrets and files: CVE-2025-68664 (deser, CVSS 9.3) leaks API keys, CVE-2026-34070 (path traversal) reads arbitrary files, CVE-2025-67644 (SQLi) hits conversation history.

findings.title: Handala (Iran MOIS) Hacks FBI Director Kash Patel's Personal Email — DOJ Confirmed
findings.affected_technology: Personal email account / US government official
findings.summary: Iran-linked MOIS group Handala leaked photos and emails stolen from FBI Director Kash Patel's personal account as retaliation for domain seizures and a $10M bounty; the FBI and DOJ both confirmed the breach and document authenticity.

findings.title: CVE-2025-12548 — Eclipse Che machine-exec Unauthenticated RCE (Metasploit Module Live)
findings.affected_technology: Eclipse Che / Red Hat OpenShift DevSpaces
findings.summary: An unauthenticated RCE in Eclipse Che's machine-exec service (WebSocket on port 3333, JSON-RPC command execution without auth) affecting Red Hat OpenShift DevSpaces now has a live Metasploit exploit module as of the 03/27 weekly release.

findings.title: CVE-2026-4681 — PTC Windchill Critical ICS Flaw, CISA Advisory, German Authorities Mobilized
findings.summary: CISA flagged a critical vulnerability in PTC Windchill, a widely deployed industrial product lifecycle management platform, with severity significant enough to mobilize German industrial cybersecurity authorities and police in response to suspected exploitation.

findings.title: Coruna / DarkSword — iOS Exploit Kit Likely Successor to Operation Triangulation
findings.summary: A new iOS exploit kit named Coruna (also referenced as DarkSword) has been identified as a likely evolution of the Operation Triangulation APT campaign, targeting iOS zero-click vulnerabilities with reported Secure Enclave access components silently addressed in iOS 18.4.1.

findings.title: watchTowr: 150 Abandoned AWS S3 Buckets — 8 Million Supply Chain Requests Intercepted
findings.affected_technology: AWS S3 / supply chain infrastructure globally
findings.summary: watchTowr Labs registered ~150 abandoned AWS S3 buckets previously used by commercial software and government CI/CD pipelines, receiving 8 million requests for unsigned binaries, VM images, and CloudFormation templates — a massive live supply chain attack surface.

findings.title: Cisco Talos: 10 TP-Link AX53 Vulns + 19 Canva Affinity Vulns (inc. RCE) + HikVision
findings.affected_technology: TP-Link Archer AX53 / Canva Affinity / HikVision
findings.summary: Cisco Talos disclosed 10 vulnerabilities in TP-Link Archer AX53 routers (including a stack-based buffer overflow CVE-2025-62673), 19 in Canva Affinity including a type-confusion RCE via crafted EMF files (CVE-2025-66342), and one in HikVision — all patched by respective vendors.

findings.title: European Commission Investigating Breach After Amazon Cloud Account Compromise
findings.summary: The European Commission launched a formal investigation after an Amazon Web Services cloud account associated with EU Commission infrastructure was hacked, with potential access to internal EU documents and communications.

findings.title: CVE-2026-23767 — Epson Network Printer ESC/POS Unauthenticated Command Injection
findings.summary: A new Metasploit module (03/27) exploits CVE-2026-23767, an unauthenticated ESC/POS command injection in networked Epson-compatible printers allowing arbitrary print command injection via crafted network packets — primarily targeting retail and POS environments.

findings.title: Port of Vigo (Spain) Disrupted by Ransomware Attack
findings.summary: A ransomware attack disrupted operations at the Port of Vigo, one of Europe's largest fishing and cargo ports, following a trend of ransomware groups targeting critical maritime infrastructure.

findings.title: HackerOne Employee Data Breach via Third-Party Navia HR Platform Hack
findings.summary: HackerOne disclosed that employee personal data was exposed after threat actors hacked Navia, a third-party HR/benefits platform, raising concerns about supply chain targeting of HR vendors to harvest researcher identity data.

findings.title: Infinite Campus Breach — ShinyHunters Claims Student Data Theft
findings.affected_technology: Infinite Campus student information system (K-12 US)
findings.summary: Infinite Campus, a student information system used across US K-12 schools, warned of a data breach after ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen student and staff data, potentially affecting millions of minors.

findings.title: Bearlyfy — Pro-Ukraine Hacktivist Group Deploys Custom Ransomware Against Russia
findings.affected_technology: Russian industrial and financial sector companies
findings.summary: A new pro-Ukraine hacktivist group, Bearlyfy, has emerged deploying custom-built ransomware against Russian companies, escalating cyber conflict on both sides of the Russia-Ukraine war.

findings.title: RedLine Malware Developer Extradited to US, Faces 30 Years
findings.summary: An alleged developer of RedLine malware — one of the most prolific credential stealers used by ransomware affiliates and cybercriminals globally — has been extradited to the United States where they face up to 30 years in prison.

findings.title: LeakBase Cybercrime Forum Admin Arrested in Russia
findings.summary: Russian authorities arrested the alleged administrator of LeakBase, a dark web forum specializing in credential data, weeks after a coordinated global law enforcement crackdown on the platform.

findings.title: CVE-2026-31381, CVE-2026-31382 — Gainsight Assist Info Disclosure + XSS (Fixed)
findings.summary: Rapid7 disclosed and fixed CVE-2026-31381 (information disclosure) and CVE-2026-31382 (XSS) in Gainsight Assist, a widely used enterprise customer success platform browser extension.

noted.summary: SANS ISC Stormcast 03/27 and BleepingComputer forum discussions reference a 'DarkSword' iOS zero-click exploit involving Secure Enclave theft, reportedly silently patched in iOS 18.4.1, but insufficient primary source confirmation — monitoring next cycle.

noted.product: AI code tools (Axon Code / prompt injection RCE)
noted.summary: OpenCVE flagged CVE-2026-30303 and CVE-2026-30304 (CVSS 9.6/9.8 AI prompt injection to arbitrary command execution) but insufficient primary source corroboration this cycle — flagged for next cycle verification.
Rapid7 published its 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report this cycle confirming 'the attack cycle is accelerating' — full read scheduled for morning cycle analysis.

Synacktiv published detailed research on cross-domain and cross-forest Resource-Based Constrained Delegation (RBCD) attacks in Active Directory environments — valuable for red team and detection engineering.
